description_long: This object is a historical biplane with a lightweight, skeletal frame and two large, parallel wings stacked one above the other. The wings are supported by a network of struts and wires, giving the structure a delicate, open appearance. The fuselage is minimal, with an exposed engine and seating area located between the wings, and the tail assembly features a horizontal stabilizer and a single vertical fin. The landing gear consists of simple skids rather than wheels, suitable for early aviation designs. The overall color scheme is a pale beige or cream, with visible metallic framework and spars. The design is reminiscent of early 20th-century aircraft, such as the Wright Flyer, emphasizing function over aerodynamic refinement.
description: A vintage biplane with a skeletal frame, dual stacked wings, and exposed engine, finished in pale beige with metallic struts and skids.
description_view_0: The front-left view highlights the open cockpit, dual wings, and the exposed engine area, with the tail and landing skids visible.
description_view_1: The left-side view emphasizes the length of the wings and the minimal fuselage, showing the strut and wire bracing clearly.
description_view_2: The rear-left view displays the tail assembly, including the horizontal stabilizer and single vertical fin, as well as the structure of the wings.
description_view_3: The front-right view provides a clear look at the cockpit area, engine, and the arrangement of the landing skids beneath the lower wing.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings connected by vertical struts and wires; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single exposed piston engine mounted at the front between the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer; no unusual features.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed skid landing gear with two main skids and no visible wheels.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open cockpit located mid-fuselage between the wings, likely single or tandem seat, with no enclosure or canopy.
